generatedFrom.note: Local-only app: the local-only gate forces store=sqlite for every entity, so no per-operation Firestore/KV split was needed. The [NON]s live beside code in src/ (spec/runes/ is empty). Access-pattern hotness is spec-derived only — no UI prototype exists.

entities.name: task
entities.purpose: A single todo item whose lifecycle (created, then completed) is recorded as append-only state history; the current task is the latest recorded state.

usedBy.does: Pure insert: generates a fresh id and appends the first state row (done=false). No read.
Point-reads the latest state by id, marks it done, then APPENDS a new state row (done=true) — never overwrites the prior state.

entities.rationale: Local-only app (single machine, embedded datastore, no cloud backend, no cross-device sync, no client-direct reads) — the local-only gate puts every entity in one embedded SQLite file so a single engine serves point-get, point-write, and the transactional append from one place. The access patterns confirm the fit: all I/O is point-keyed by exact id with no query/list/sort/pagination (which would pull toward Firestore) and no cross-process hot atomics/sessions (which would pull toward Deno KV). SQLite transactions give the per-task monotonic seq the append-only model needs.
entities.key: Logical identity = task.id. Physical storage key = composite primary key (task_id, seq) on the append-only `task_state` child table, where seq is a monotonic per-task version (1 = creation). The current state for an id is the row with MAX(seq) for that task_id.

document._note: Logical view of one task id with its append-only state history. Physically these are rows in the `task_state` child table; `currentState` is the folded TaskDto returned to callers (highest seq).

accessPatterns.hotness: unknown (spec-derived; no UI prototype). Assumed low on a single machine. Transactional INSERT of seq=1.

accessPatterns.operation: task.complete — load current state by id
accessPatterns.hotness: unknown (spec-derived). Exact-id lookup folded to the MAX(seq) row; no scan.
unknown (spec-derived). Read-modify-write on the same id; seq = MAX(seq)+1 assigned inside the same transaction as the INSERT so concurrent completes cannot collide on a seq or lose history.

indexes.name: task_state_pk
indexes.why: Primary key. Serves the current-state read (WHERE task_id=? ORDER BY seq DESC LIMIT 1) and guarantees one row per (task, version).

collection.name: task_state
collection.realization: child table (SQLite local) — one row per state transition; `seq` is a monotonic per-task version assigned within the write transaction. markDone() appends a new row; it must never UPDATE an existing row.

immutability.currentStateOnRead: SELECT title, done FROM task_state WHERE task_id = ? ORDER BY seq DESC LIMIT 1 — the highest-seq row is the task's current state (the TaskDto returned by load/toDto).
immutability.why: spec.md 'The heart of the design': completing a task records a NEW state rather than overwriting the old one, and the latest state is the current one. The existing data adapter's `db.put(\`task:${id}\`)` key-overwrite is exactly what this forbids — the design records that the boundary must append a child row, not overwrite. (Reconciling the .rune/code to this is the reconciler/build stage, not this design.)

retention.why: Append-only domain history: every state transition IS the audit trail of the task's lifecycle and the source of current state, so nothing may expire. spec.md non-goals also state 'No deletion'. A TTL here would silently delete the very history the append-only model exists to keep.

notes: One module-scoped entity (`task`). Local-only gate applied per the orchestrator's confirmed verdict: store=sqlite, no projections. Append-only history applied per the confirmed product heart: task.complete appends a `task_state` child row rather than mutating in place, so the data boundary that today overwrites `task:<id>` must be reconciled to append (handled downstream by the reconciler/build, not here). Validate against the tasks spec only (src/tasks/tasks.rune); src/ as a whole also contains an unrelated `[NON] note` from the notes module, which is out of scope for this design.
